article_title: ShinyHunters Claims Near-Petabyte Data Theft from Telus Digital After Breaching BPO Giant Through Stolen Cloud Credentials

content_preview.title: ShinyHunters Claims Near-Petabyte Data Theft from Telus Digital After Breaching BPO Giant Through Stolen Cloud Credentials
content_preview.summary: Telus Digital confirmed a breach after ShinyHunters claimed to have stolen up to one petabyte of data using cloud credentials obtained in a prior third-party compromise.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Telus Digital, the digital experience subsidiary of Canadian telecommunications giant Telus, confirmed on March 12 that it suffered a security breach after the ShinyHunters cybercriminal group claimed to have stolen close to one petabyte of data from the company's systems. The incident, first reported by [The Register](https://www.theregister.com/2026/03/15/telus_breach_starbucks_attack/), ranks among the largest data thefts ever attributed to a single threat actor and exposes the c...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured cybersecurity article with clear attack chain description and broader implications.
editor_notes.source_verification: The Register confirms breach, GCP credentials from Salesloft, petabyte-scale claim, and Telus 'limited systems' response. BleepingComputer returned 403 (article exists but inaccessible for verification). TechRadar headline confirms breach and data volume. Some specific details (trufflehog tool, $65M ransom, FBI background checks, Reuters data verification) could not be independently verified from the accessible source content — they may be in the BleepingComputer/TechRadar full texts that weren't parseable.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core narrative (ShinyHunters breached Telus Digital via Salesloft-derived GCP credentials, near-petabyte claim) verified via The Register. Specific details attributable to BleepingComputer and TechRadar could not be independently checked due to 403/parsing limitations.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Appropriately cautious language ('claims', 'reportedly').
editor_notes.originality: Distinct from existing ShinyHunters/Salesforce article (March 11) — different breach, different victim, different attack vector.
concerns: Some specific claims (trufflehog, $65M ransom, FBI background checks) could not be verified from accessible source content
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid cybersecurity reporting on a significant breach. Core facts verified. Approved despite some unverifiable details in inaccessible sources.
